purpose: Any implementation claiming JA-ES conformance should reproduce every sha256 below before it is trusted to seal or verify records. Cross-language agreement on canonicalization is the single assumption the entire integrity model rests on, and the one most likely to be silently wrong. The utf16-vs-codepoint-ordering and negative-zero vectors are deliberately discriminating: a plausible, working, BMP-only implementation passes everything else and fails these.

vectors.name: scalars-and-key-order
vectors.note: Keys sort by UTF-16 code unit. Integers carry no decimal point.

vectors.name: utf16-vs-codepoint-ordering
vectors.note: DISCRIMINATING. U+1D400 (supplementary, surrogate pair D835 DC00) sorts BEFORE U+FFFD under UTF-16 code-unit ordering, and AFTER it under code-point ordering. RFC 8785 requires UTF-16. An implementation sorting by code point produces a different canonical form and a different digest. Vectors confined to the BMP cannot detect this error.

vectors.name: negative-zero
vectors.note: Raw IEEE-754 negative zero. RFC 8785 serializes it as 0, not -0.

vectors.name: number-formatting
vectors.note: ECMAScript Number::toString. Integers carry no decimal point; 1e21 is the threshold above which exponential form is used; 1e-7 is the threshold below which it is used.

vectors.name: string-escaping-controls
vectors.note: Control characters below U+0020 use \u form except where a short escape exists. Non-ASCII printable characters are NOT escaped.

vectors.name: string-escaping
vectors.note: Only the mandatory escapes; no gratuitous \u sequences for printable characters.

vectors.name: arrays-preserve-order
vectors.note: Array order is data and must not be sorted.

vectors.name: empty-containers
vectors.note: Empty object and array serialize without whitespace.

vectors.name: ja-es-record-hash
vectors.note: The JA-ES rule end to end: remove record_hash and signature from integrity, canonicalize, SHA-256. Expected digest equals the record's sealed integrity.record_hash.
